MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Crystal L. Van Der Putten, Esq., of Livingston Law Firm, Tel. 925.952.9880, appearing for Defendant and moving party.
Margaret A. Thurn, Esq., of Basile Law Firm, Tel. 805.781.8600, appearing for plaintiff and opposing party via court call.

The parties submitted to the tentative ruling. The court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS DR. PEPPER SNAPPLE GROUP, INC., THE AMERICAN BOTTLING COMPANY and SPLASH TRANSPORT, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is Granted. The second cause of action is not a distinct claim from the first cause of action because the sole basis that moving defendants can be liable is due to any negligence committed by Mr. Hurtado. Defendants' binding admission of vicarious liability for any negligence of Mr. Hurtado makes it unnecessary for any determination to be made as to plaintiff's theories of negligent supervision, training and entrustment. No permissible purpose is served by retaining the second cause of action. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported.
